steps.title: Use vertex form of quadratic
steps.content: The quadratic models a projectile's height, reaching maximum at vertex. Vertex form: $$ h(t) = a(t - 6)^2 + 576 $$. The vertex (6, 576) represents the max height at 6 seconds.

steps.title: Find coefficient 'a' using initial condition
steps.content: At $ t = 0 $, height is 0: $$ 0 = a(0 - 6)^2 + 576 $$. Solving: $$ 0 = 36a + 576 \Rightarrow a = -16 $$. Equation becomes: $ h(t) = -16(t - 6)^2 + 576 $.

steps.title: Calculate height at t = 10 seconds
steps.content: Substitute $ t = 10 $: $$ h(10) = -16(4)^2 + 576 = -256 + 576 = 320 $$. The answer is 320.
